You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/runs/0013/sessions/0022-horizon-T9

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

Title: Jacobian⇄Albanese endgame glue
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/*.lean,blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese*.tex,blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex

$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$

# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.
- **Cheaper architectural alternative** if the instance stays stuck: the only consumer is `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` at line 1545 (`cech_flatBaseChange`, affine S/S'), applied to the Čech complex whose terms are iso to tildes — a complex-specific homology-commutation via `tilde`-exactness (`TildeExactness.lean`) + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` could replace the general instance, at the cost of a termwise-tilde chain-iso.
- **A.3 Pic0 cone state after run-0008 T5 — supersedes the run-0005 'IdentityComponent all 9 sorries blocked' memory.**

- **Proved sorry-free**: `Pic0.grpObj` (`Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))` = `isSubgroupHomomorphism (PicScheme C)`; instances synthesize directly from `groupSchemeStructure` (CommGrpObj parent projection) + the `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` projection instance; `Pic0Scheme` unfolds by rfl, plain `exact` works). 6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# Function-field pullback of a rational map
10	
11	For a rational map `f : X ⤏ Y` out of an integral scheme `X`, Mathlib's
12	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField` gives a morphism
13	`Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` (the restriction of `f` to the generic point). Composing it with
14	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo` produces the pullback of germs
15	`𝒪_{Y, f(η_X)} ⟶ K(X)` at the image of the generic point of `X`.
16	
17	When `f` is **dominant** and `Y` is irreducible, that image point is the generic
18	point of `Y`, so the germ pullback becomes a field homomorphism
19	`K(Y) ⟶ K(X)` — the function-field functoriality `K(Y) → K(X)` that Milne's
20	rational-map extension leg (`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`) and the
21	Weil-divisor obstruction (`thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`) both require.
22	
23	## Main definitions
24	
25	* `Scheme.RationalMap.stalkPullback` — the germ pullback
26	  `𝒪_{Y, f(η_X)} ⟶ K(X)` (no dominance needed).
27	* `Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` — for a dominant
28	  rational map into an irreducible target, `f(η_X) = η_Y`.
29	* `Scheme.RationalMap.functionFieldPullback` — the induced field homomorphism
30	  `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)` for a dominant rational map.
31	-/
32	
33	set_option autoImplicit false
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace IsLocalRing
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	namespace Scheme
42	
43	namespace RationalMap
44	
45	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
46	
47	/-- **Germ pullback along a rational map.** For a rational map `f : X ⤏ Y` out of
48	an integral scheme `X`, the associated morphism `f.fromFunctionField : Spec K(X) ⟶ Y`
49	induces, by `Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo`, a local ring homomorphism from the germ
50	of `Y` at the image `f(η_X)` of the generic point of `X` into the function field
51	`K(X)`. -/
52	noncomputable def stalkPullback [IsIntegral X] (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
53	    Y.presheaf.stalk (f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField)) ⟶ X.functionField :=
54	  Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField
55	
56	/-!
57	## Dominant refinement (planned)
58	
59	When `f` is **dominant** and `Y` is irreducible, the base point
60	`f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X))` equals `genericPoint Y`, so `stalkPullback`
61	becomes a field homomorphism `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)`:

63	* `fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint`: reduce to a representative `g` via

67	  sends `closedPoint` to `genericPoint g.domain.toScheme` (identify the inner
68	  point via `Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι` + `Scheme.fromSpecStalk_closedPoint`,
69	  `g.domain.ι` injective; the domain's generic point via
70	  `genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion g.domain.ι`); then
71	  `(genericPoint_spec _).image g.hom.continuous` with `hdom.denseRange.closure_range`
72	  and `IsGenericPoint.eq` (schemes are T0) give `= genericPoint Y`.
73	* `functionFieldPullback := (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (Inseparable.of_eq …)).hom ≫ f.stalkPullback`.

9	/-!
10	# The Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (A.4.d)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.d** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages Milne's
14	*Abelian Varieties* §III.6 Proposition 6.1: for a complete nonsingular curve
15	`C` of genus `g > 0` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked
16	`k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the Abel–Jacobi morphism
17	`ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` is the **Albanese morphism** of the pointed curve
18	`(C, P₀)`: any pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety with
19	`φ(P₀) = η_A` factors uniquely as `φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}` for a homomorphism of
20	group schemes `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.
21	
22	The chapter commits to the **symmetric-power route** (cube-free): the
23	symmetric assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` factors through
24	`Sym^g C`, the resulting morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` descends through
25	the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` to a rational map
26	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, and Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (`extend_to_av` in the sibling
27	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c) promotes it to a regular
28	morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The group-homomorphism property of `ψ` then

211	/-! ## §1. The Abel–Jacobi morphism at a marked point
212	
213	The first sub-lemma packages the construction and elementary regularity
214	of the Abel–Jacobi morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` at the basepoint
215	`P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`. On `k̄`-points,
216	`ι_{P₀}(Q) = [𝓞_C(Q − P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`; at the basepoint
217	itself `ι_{P₀}(P₀) = η_J`.
218	
219	This is the content gated on A.3 (existence of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` as a
220	representable group scheme); the morphism is the moduli classifier of
221	the rigidified diagonal correspondence
222	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})` on `C × C`, taken as a
223	relative degree-zero line bundle on `C × C` over the second factor.
224	
225	Blueprint reference: `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism` (Milne, *Abelian
226	Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1 and Summary III.6.11). -/

229

231	algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked `k̄`-point

233	morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` characterised on `T`-points by the

235	Poincaré–Picard correspondence on `C × Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` and
236	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})`.

253	
254	The `g`-th symmetric power of `C` is the quotient of the `g`-fold
255	Cartesian power `C^g` by the natural permutation action of the
256	symmetric group `S_g`. Mathlib `b80f227` has no formalised symmetric

258	modules — so the scheme-theoretic construction must be supplied
259	project-side, following Milne's recipe of §III.3 Proposition 3.1: on
260	each open affine `U = Spec A ⊆ C` form `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`, then glue
261	along the standard open-cover patching (Mumford 1970 §II.7 / §III.11).
262

304	The second sub-lemma is the elementary fact that the symmetric
305	assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` from `C^g` to `A`

309	scheme; combined with the universal property of `Sym^g C`, this yields
310	the unique factorisation.
311	
312	Blueprint reference: `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` (Milne's proof of

358	dominant with one-dimensional fibres, and its generic fibre is a single
359	`k̄`-point (the linear system `|D|` of a general degree-`g` divisor `D`
360	on a curve of genus `g` is `0`-dimensional by Riemann–Roch).

376	
377	The final auxiliary lemma packages the two-step descent that promotes
378	`Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` (after passing through the birational morphism
379	`f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`) to a regular morphism

381	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A` on the dense open of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` on which
382	`f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism via
383	Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (the sibling `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`).
384	This is the unique step of the proof that consumes A.4.c.
385	
386	Blueprint reference: `lem:descent_through_birational_sigma` (Milne's
387	proof of III.6.1, p. 104, "It therefore defines a rational map
388	`ψ : J → A`, which (I 3.2) shows to be a regular map"). -/

390	/-- **Descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.**
391	
392	There is a *unique* regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
393	`symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ (genus C) ≫ ψ`
394	(equivalently, `ψ` is the unique regular extension of the rational map
395	`Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1} : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`).
396	
397	The rational map is constructed on the dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
398	where `f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism
399	on all of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` via Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (sibling
400	`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` in
401	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c). Uniqueness is the
402	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle: two regular
403	morphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` agreeing on a dense open are equal.
404	
405	iter-178+: body invokes `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` on the
406	rational map produced by inverting `f^{(g)}` on its dense open of
407	isomorphism. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed

419	/-! ## §5.5. Albanese ⇔ symmetric-power equation (connecting biconditional)
420	
421	The iter-182 structural decomposition extracts the precise *connecting
422	identity* between the Albanese-form factorisation
423	`φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ` and the symmetric-power-form factorisation
424	`symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀
425	(genus C) ≫ ψ` as a named typed-`sorry` biconditional helper. With this
426	biconditional in hand the body of `albanese_universal_property` reduces
427	to sorry-free assembly from `descentThroughBirationalSigma`. -/
428	
429	/-- **Connecting biconditional: Albanese-form equation ⇔ symmetric-power-form
430	equation.**
431	
432	For every candidate descent morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`, the Albanese-form
433	factorisation `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ` holds iff the symmetric-power-form
434	factorisation `symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian

460	/-! ## §6. The Albanese universal property — headline theorem
461	
462	The headline result of the chapter: for every pointed morphism
463	`φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with `φ(P₀) = η_A`, there
464	exists a unique regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
465	`φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}`. The proof follows Milne Proposition III.6.1
466	verbatim, using the four sub-lemmas above plus Milne Theorem I.3.2
467	(`extend_to_av`) and Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
468	consequence).
469	
470	Blueprint reference: `thm:albanese_universal_property` (Milne,
471	*Abelian Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1, p. 104). -/
472	
473	/-- **Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` — Milne III §6
474	Proposition 6.1.**
475	
476	For a complete nonsingular curve `C` of genus `g > 0` over an
477	algebraically closed field `k̄`, a marked `k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, and
478	every pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with
479	`φ(P₀) = η_A`, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
480	`ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that `φ = ι_{P₀} ≫ ψ`.
481	
482	Proof sketch (Milne III.6.1):
483	1. **Symmetrisation.** `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` is
484	   `S_g`-symmetric (`A` commutative), so factors through
485	   `Sym^g C` as `Sym^g φ` (`lem:symmetric_product_av_map`).
486	2. **Descent to a regular morphism on `J`.** The birational morphism
487	   `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (`lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`)
488	   exhibits `Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1}` as a rational map
489	   `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, which by Milne Theorem I.3.2 extends uniquely to
490	   a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`
491	   (`lem:descent_through_birational_sigma`).
492	3. **Factorisation `ψ ∘ ι_{P₀} = φ`.** Use Milne's identification of
493	   `ι_{P₀}` with the composite `Q ↦ Q + (g − 1) P₀` followed by
494	   `f^{(g)}`; together with `φ(P₀) = η_A` and the additive structure
495	   of `A`, this gives `ψ(ι_{P₀}(Q)) = φ(Q)` on `k̄`-points, hence
496	   globally by the reduced-and-separated agreement principle.
497	4. **Homomorphism property.** `ψ` sends `η_J = ι_{P₀}(P₀)` to
498	   `φ(P₀) = η_A`, so by Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
499	   consequence) it is a homomorphism of group schemes.
500	5. **Uniqueness.** Two homomorphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` with the same
501	   restriction to `ι_{P₀}(C)` agree on the `g`-fold sum
502	   `ι_{P₀}(C) + ⋯ + ι_{P₀}(C) = Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (since `f^{(g)}` is
503	   surjective), hence are equal.

/-!
## Dominant refinement

When `f` is **dominant** and `Y` is irreducible, the base point
`f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X))` equals `genericPoint Y`, so `stalkPullback`
becomes a field homomorphism `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)`.
-/

/-- **For a dominant rational map into an irreducible target, the function-field
morphism sends the closed point of `Spec K(X)` to the generic point of `Y`.**

Reducing to a representative partial map `g`, the morphism
`g.fromFunctionField = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (η_X) ≫ g.hom` sends the closed
point of `Spec 𝒪_{X, η_X} = Spec K(X)` to `g.hom (η_{g.domain})`. The inner point
is the generic point of the domain (checked after applying the open immersion
`g.domain.ι`, using `Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι` and `fromSpecStalk_closedPoint`),
and a dominant morphism sends the generic point of its (irreducible) source to the
generic point of `Y` (image of a generic point is generic on the dense range, and
generic points are unique in the T₀ space `Y`). -/
theorem fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
    f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField) = genericPoint Y := by
  obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
  haveI : IsDominant g.hom := (PartialMap.isDominant_toRationalMap_iff g).mp ‹_›
  have hη : genericPoint X ∈ g.domain :=
    (genericPoint_specializes _).mem_open g.domain.2 g.dense_domain.nonempty.choose_spec
  haveI : Nonempty (g.domain : Type _) := ⟨⟨genericPoint X, hη⟩⟩
  haveI : IsIntegral g.domain.toScheme := inferInstance
  haveI : IrreducibleSpace g.domain.toScheme := inferInstance
  -- reduce the rational-map `fromFunctionField` to the partial-map one, then to the
  -- composite `g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (η_X) hη ≫ g.hom`
  rw [RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]
  have hcomp : PartialMap.fromFunctionField g
      = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hη ≫ g.hom := rfl
  rw [hcomp, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
  -- the inner point is the generic point of the domain
  have hp : (g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hη) (closedPoint X.functionField)
      = genericPoint g.domain.toScheme := by
    apply (Scheme.Opens.ι g.domain).isOpenEmbedding.injective
    rw [genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion (Scheme.Opens.ι g.domain),
      ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι]
    exact Scheme.fromSpecStalk_closedPoint
  rw [hp]
  -- a dominant morphism sends the generic point of an irreducible source to `genericPoint Y`
  have himg := (genericPoint_spec g.domain.toScheme).image g.hom.continuous
  rw [Set.image_univ, g.hom.denseRange.closure_range] at himg
  exact himg.eq (genericPoint_spec Y)

/-- **The induced field homomorphism `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)` of a dominant rational map.**

Since `f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X)) = genericPoint Y`
(`fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint`), the germ pullback `stalkPullback` has
source `𝒪_{Y, η_Y} = K(Y)` up to the canonical stalk isomorphism, giving the
function-field functoriality `K(Y) → K(X)` required by the rational-map extension
leg of Milne's Albanese argument. -/
noncomputable def functionFieldPullback [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
    Y.functionField ⟶ X.functionField :=
  (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr
    (Inseparable.of_eq (fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint f).symm)).hom ≫ f.stalkPullback

text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.claude/skills/blueprint-conventions

Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.

## Environments

Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
its following `proof` environment form one node's content.

## Per-node macros

- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
  proof.
- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).

## House style

- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
  its proof stays complete.
- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.

\section{Milne Theorem 3.1: codimension-\(\geq 2\) extension}
\label{sec:milne_thm31}

Before the extension theorem itself we record the function-field functoriality
\(K(Y) \to K(X)\) attached to a dominant rational map, which the codim-\(1\) step of
the extension proof uses to view a representative as a morphism \(\Spec K(X) \to Y\).

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Image of the closed point of \(\Spec K(X)\)]
  \label{lem:fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint}
  Let \(X\) be an integral scheme and \(Y\) an irreducible scheme, and let
  \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a dominant rational map. The induced morphism
  \(\Spec K(X) \to Y\) (the restriction of \(f\) to the generic point of \(X\))
  sends the closed point of \(\Spec K(X)\) to the generic point of \(Y\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}\leanok
  Choose a representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\); its underlying morphism
  \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) is dominant. The morphism \(\Spec K(X) \to Y\) factors as
  \(\Spec \mathcal O_{X,\eta_X} \to U \xrightarrow{\varphi_U} Y\), where the first map
  sends the closed point to the generic point \(\eta_U\) of \(U\) (checked after the open
  immersion \(U \hookrightarrow X\), which identifies \(\eta_U\) with \(\eta_X\)). A
  dominant morphism carries the generic point of its irreducible source to a generic
  point of \(Y\); as \(Y\) is a \(T_0\) space its generic point is unique, so the image
  is \(\eta_Y\).
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}\leanok
  \label{def:functionFieldPullback}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.functionFieldPullback}
  \uses{lem:fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint}
  For a dominant rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) from an integral scheme
  \(X\) to an irreducible scheme \(Y\), the \textbf{function-field pullback} is the ring
  homomorphism \(K(Y) \to K(X)\) obtained from the germ pullback
  \(\mathcal O_{Y, f(\eta_X)} \to K(X)\) of \(f\) at the generic point, identified along
  \cref{lem:fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint} with the stalk
  \(\mathcal O_{Y,\eta_Y} = K(Y)\).
\end{definition}

The first input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is the following: a rational map from a normal
variety to a \emph{complete} variety automatically extends across every codim-\(1\)
point.

All work is verified, committed, and documented. Here's my report.

## Summary

Completed and kernel-verified the **function-field pullback bridge `K(Y) → K(X)`** for dominant rational maps — the substrate the memory `t9-albanese-endgame-unblock-map` had tracked as a partial ("stalkPullback landed, dominant refinement documented but walled"). Both new declarations are axiom-clean, committed, and pinned in the blueprint 1-to-1. This is the substep-3 function-field functoriality on the extension-leg critical path (Milne §I.3).

## Progress
- AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/RationalMapFunctionField.lean: added `fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` + `functionFieldPullback`, both axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`; file kernel-compiles (`lake env lean`, exit 0); reachable from root (`AlgebraicJacobian.lean:103`).
- blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex: added `lem:fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` + `def:functionFieldPullback`, complete proofs, `\leanok`, honest `\uses`; labels unique.
- AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean: no change — 7 sorries remain, all gated on the missing Sym^g scheme substrate + Pic0 representability (out of scope).
- AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean: no change — the ONE Milne-3.3 sorry (`:1721`) remains; its difference map needs new RationalMap primitives (see below).

## Issues
- **Extension leg not axiom-clean.** `extend_to_av` (Thm32) still carries the Milne-3.3 `sorry` transitively. Also, CodimOneExtension imports `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (15 sorries); I did not audit whether the specific AB lemmas it uses are themselves clean — an open soundness question worth a targeted axiom-check.
- **Blueprint drift (pre-existing, unfixed):** `thm:codim_one_extension`'s `\uses{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}` + proof prose still describe the 0AVF/Cohen–Macaulay route that memory says is a phantom in the honest valuative chain. Flagged in memory bullet 20; left as consistency debt.
- I verified the changed leaf file at kernel level but did not run the full multi-thousand-job `lake build` (unnecessary: RationalMapFunctionField is Mathlib-only, imported only by the root aggregator, and compiles standalone — no downstream dependents can break).

## Why I stopped
Task not complete: the Albanese UP / endgame glue remains gated on two large greenfield sub-builds, neither one-session work.
- I carried the one concrete, completable critical-path brick to a verified, committed finish.
- The next brick (Milne 3.3 substep 1, the difference map `Φ = m∘(f×f) : X×X ⇢ G`) requires **new `RationalMap` infrastructure that mathlib lacks**: I confirmed `Birational/RationalMap.lean` exposes only `compHom` (right-compose with a morphism) — no rational-map products and no precomposition with a dominant/open morphism. Building those + the germ-definedness criterion + substep 4b is a genuine multi-session build, not finishable-and-verifiable now.
- Starting it would leave broken/half-built infrastructure — worse than a clean verified stop.

## Next
- Build the RationalMap primitives: precomposition with an open dominant morphism (needs "preimage of a dense open under an open map is dense") and a pairing into a product target; then the difference map `Φ`, the germ-definedness criterion, and substep 4b to close Milne 3.3 (`CodimOneExtension.lean:1721`).
- Independently: the UP leg needs the Sym^g scheme substrate (Milne III.3, glue `Spec((A^{⊗g})^{Sₘ})`) — the larger of the two greenfield blocks.
- Cheap follow-up: axiom-check whether the AuslanderBuchsbaum lemmas that CodimOneExtension actually uses are clean, to bound the extension leg's taint precisely.
